title: Improve takt telemetry: per-bead breakdown and cost tracking
description:
When 'takt telemetry --feature-root <id>' is used, the output shows aggregate stats but no per-bead breakdown. Additionally, cost_usd is never aggregated or displayed in the CLI output, even though it is tracked in telemetry artifacts and rendered in the TUI.

Changes needed (all in src/agent_takt/cli.py):

1. aggregate_telemetry() — sum cost_usd from telemetry artifacts (use _collect_bead_telemetry per bead) and include total_cost_usd and avg_cost_usd_per_bead in the returned dict.

2. _format_telemetry_table() — when filters['feature_root'] is set, render a per-bead table showing: bead_id, agent_type, status, wall-clock duration, cost_usd for each bead in the filtered set. Pass the bead list into this function.

3. command_telemetry() — pass the filtered bead list to _format_telemetry_table so it can render per-bead rows.

4. Add cost lines to the aggregate section of _format_telemetry_table (total cost and avg cost per bead) in both scoped and unscoped views.
acceptance_criteria: takt telemetry --feature-root <id> shows a per-bead table with bead_id, agent_type, status, duration, and cost_usd columns
takt telemetry (unscoped) shows total_cost_usd and avg_cost_usd_per_bead in the aggregate section
cost_usd values read from telemetry artifacts match what the TUI shows
takt telemetry --json includes cost fields in the aggregates block

handoff_summary.completed: All four acceptance criteria addressed: per-bead breakdown table in scoped view, total/avg cost in aggregate section (both scoped and unscoped), cost_usd reads from the same bead.metadata["telemetry"] source used by the TUI, and cost fields included in --json aggregates output.
handoff_summary.risks: cost_usd values are sourced from bead.metadata["telemetry"], which is the lightweight in-bead copy written by the scheduler. Beads that completed before the telemetry write was introduced will show N/A for cost.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify: per-bead table renders for --feature-root, aggregate cost fields appear in both scoped and unscoped output, --json output includes cost fields, and N/A is shown gracefully when no cost data exists.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — all changes are confined to cli.py, which no other in-progress bead claims.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Cost is read from bead.metadata["telemetry"]["cost_usd"] (the lightweight in-bead copy) rather than from telemetry artifact files on disk. This is consistent with how the TUI reads cost and avoids duplicate I/O. The per-bead breakdown table is only rendered in the human-readable view when --feature-root is active (not in --json mode, since the beads array already carries cost_usd per entry). The beads param to _format_telemetry_table is None for the unscoped view to preserve existing output.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Key paths to test: (1) takt telemetry with beads that have cost_usd in metadata — verify totals and per-bead rows; (2) takt telemetry with beads missing cost data — verify N/A shown correctly; (3) takt telemetry --json with --feature-root — verify cost fields in aggregates and beads entries; (4) takt telemetry without --feature-root — verify no per-bead table but cost aggregate lines appear.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Beads completed before scheduler started writing telemetry metadata will have cost_usd=None and show as N/A. The per-bead breakdown is only shown in the human-readable output when --feature-root is specified; unscoped view shows only aggregate cost lines.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — all changes are confined to cli.py, which no other in-progress bead claims.

execution_history.summary: Added per-bead cost tracking and per-bead breakdown table to takt telemetry CLI output. Four targeted changes to src/agent_takt/cli.py: (1) new _bead_cost_usd() helper reads cost_usd from bead.metadata["telemetry"]; (2) aggregate_telemetry() now accumulates cost_usd values and returns total_cost_usd and avg_cost_usd_per_bead; (3) _format_telemetry_table() now accepts an optional beads list, appends Total/Avg cost lines to the aggregate section, and renders a per-bead table (bead_id, agent_type, status, duration, cost_usd) when feature_root filter is active; (4) command_telemetry() passes beads to _format_telemetry_table when --feature-root is set, and includes cost_usd in the JSON beads list.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
